title: Reusable Workflows, Composite Actions, Interfaces, and Permission Flow · CI/CD Retrieval
summary: An operations handoff reconstructs reusable workflows, composite actions, interfaces, and permission flow from delayed run evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the versioned reusable automation contract under changed load. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned reusable automation contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose reusable workflow, composite action, JavaScript action, Docker action, script, or package based on job boundary, portability, testing, and authority.
Define workflow_call inputs, secrets, outputs, validation, defaults, required values, and failure semantics as a versioned interface.
Trace caller and called-workflow permissions, secrets inheritance, environment secret boundaries, nested depth, and non-escalation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Shared Automation Evolution
steps.instruction:
review case — A review reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership.

Inspect the Ci Shared Automation Evolution trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Shared Automation Evolution.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Shared Automation Evolution, keep Ci Shared Automation Evolution connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: For Ci Shared Automation Evolution, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Shared Automation Evolution, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Shared Automation Evolution precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Shared Automation Evolution, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A review reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Shared Automation Evolution, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Centralizing a workflow automatically eliminates configuration drift.

stimulus.title: Ci Shared Automation Evolution case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Centralizing a workflow automatically eliminates configuration drift.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership. Artifact under review: versioned reusable automation contract.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Ci Shared Automation Evolution, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof.
